Deadlocks can occur when transactions wait on each other in a cycle. PostgreSQL detects deadlocks and cancels one transaction so the others can continue. Applications should keep transactions short and acquire locks in a consistent order to reduce deadlock risk.

After a class statement runs, the class object supports attribute reference and instantiation. Attribute reference reads values such as methods or class variables from the class namespace. Instantiation calls the class object like a function and creates a new instance object. If the class defines init, Python calls it during instantiation to initialize the instance state.

FastAPI dependency injection lets an endpoint declare reusable requirements instead of creating every object directly in the route body. A dependency is usually a callable that receives request data or other dependencies and returns a value for the endpoint. This pattern keeps route functions focused on the HTTP operation while shared concerns stay in small functions.

Repeatable Read gives a transaction a stable snapshot for its duration. Queries in the same transaction continue to see the same committed state from the snapshot, even if other transactions commit changes later. This reduces surprises from repeated reads but may require retrying a transaction when serialization conflicts occur.

final_evidence_units.text: FastAPI includes dependency information in OpenAPI when it affects parameters or security. This allows generated API docs to show required query parameters, headers, cookies, and security schemes that come from dependency declarations.

A dependency can depend on another dependency. FastAPI resolves this tree and reuses results where appropriate during one request. Nested dependencies make it possible to build layered behavior, such as authentication that relies on token parsing and user loading.

PureLink validates uploads before processing. Supported local demo text formats include Markdown-like text, Markdown, PDF, DOCX, and other configured extensions. Upload validation also checks size and MIME settings such as MAXUPLOADSIZEMB, ALLOWEDUPLOADEXTENSIONS, and ALLOWEDUPLOADMIMETYPES.

cases.question: PostgreSQL MVCC 是什么？

final_evidence_units.text:
Source basis: PostgreSQL official documentation, MVCC and locking

Adaptation: concise paraphrase for deterministic PureLink evaluation

PostgreSQL uses multiversion concurrency control, or MVCC, so readers and writers can often operate without blocking each other. Each transaction sees a snapshot of rows that are visible according to its isolation level. Updates create new row versions instead of overwriting the only copy immediately, which helps preserve consistency while concurrent work continues.

Parser routing chooses a parser based on file type and available providers. Text files remain sourcetype=text, while Markdown-like .txt content can be conservatively recognized as structured text. The parser produces extracted content that later becomes document blocks, chunks, citation units, and indexes.

DocumentBlock records preserve structure such as headings, text blocks, tables, code blocks, page metadata, and source spans. Blocks make it possible for later chunking and citation generation to keep section and locator context instead of treating every document as one flat string.

final_evidence_units.text: Deadlocks can occur when transactions wait on each other in a cycle. PostgreSQL detects deadlocks and cancels one transaction so the others can continue. Applications should keep transactions short and acquire locks in a consistent order to reduce deadlock risk.

PureLink validates uploads before processing. Supported local demo text formats include Markdown-like text, Markdown, PDF, DOCX, and other configured extensions. Upload validation also checks size and MIME settings such as MAXUPLOADSIZEMB, ALLOWEDUPLOADEXTENSIONS, and ALLOWEDUPLOADMIMETYPES.

PostgreSQL uses multiversion concurrency control, or MVCC, so readers and writers can often operate without blocking each other. Each transaction sees a snapshot of rows that are visible according to its isolation level. Updates create new row versions instead of overwriting the only copy immediately, which helps preserve consistency while concurrent work continues.

Aurora Mini is the compact member of the Aurora catalog. It is designed for people who need a lightweight device that can move between desks, meeting rooms, and travel bags. Its silver color and 1.2 kg weight distinguish it from the larger Pro and Server models.

Deadlocks can occur when transactions wait on each other in a cycle. PostgreSQL detects deadlocks and cancels one transaction so the others can continue. Applications should keep transactions short and acquire locks in a consistent order to reduce deadlock risk.

FastAPI dependency injection lets an endpoint declare reusable requirements instead of creating every object directly in the route body. A dependency is usually a callable that receives request data or other dependencies and returns a value for the endpoint. This pattern keeps route functions focused on the HTTP operation while shared concerns stay in small functions.

Inheritance lets a class derive behavior from another class. A subclass can reuse parent methods, add new behavior, or override methods to specialize behavior. Method lookup starts on the instance's class and continues through base classes, so inheritance can express shared interfaces while allowing specialization.

Database sessions are a common dependency. A session dependency can open the database session, yield it to the endpoint, and close it after the request. This avoids repeating session setup code in every endpoint and gives tests a single seam for replacing database behavior.
